<<if statement>> <<to switch>> <<control flow>> <<variant>> <<is required>> <<for the>> <<given conditional>> <<structure>>. <<The task>> <<involves>> <<replacing>> <<the if-else>> <<chain>> <<with a>> <<switch statement>> <<while>> <<ensuring>> <<optimal efficiency>>. <<This transformation>> <<must be>> <<done carefully>> <<to avoid>> <<loss of>> <<functionality>> <<or performance>> <<degradation>>.

<<Here is>> <<the equivalent>> <<switch version>> <<of the>> <<original code>>:

```javascript
switch (grade) {
    case "A":
        gradeValue = 4.0;
        break;
    case "B":
        gradeValue = 3.0;
        break;
    case "C":
        gradeValue = 2.0;
        break;
    case "D":
        gradeValue = 1.0;
        break;
}
```

<<The switch>> <<statement>> <<is more>> <<efficient>> <<than multiple>> <<else if>> <<conditions>> <<when>> <<checking>> <<for exact>> <<string matches>> <<as in this case>>. <<Each case>> <<is evaluated>> <<in a>> <<lookup table>> <<style>> <<which is>> <<faster>> <<than sequential>> <<if-else checks>>.

<<This approach>> <<also improves>> <<readability>> <<and maintainability>> <<of the code>> <<by grouping>> <<related conditions>> <<together>>. <<The use>> <<of break statements>> <<ensures>> <<that the>> <<switch does not>> <<fall through>> <<to the>> <<next case>> <<by accident>>.

<<In conclusion>> <<the switch>> <<version is>> <<both more>> <<efficient and>> <<cleaner>> <<than the>> <<original if-else>> <<structure>>. <<It adheres>> <<to the>> <<constraints of>> <<the task>> <<and provides>> <<a better>> <<control flow>> <<solution>>.
